# EvoClaw v1.0.0-beta.1

First public beta of EvoClaw 1.0. Pre-built binaries are available for macOS, Linux, and Windows — no Rust toolchain required.

---

## What's New in v1.0.0-beta.1

### Full TUI Rewrite
- Replaced the old scrolling-stdout output with a persistent event-driven terminal UI
- Fixed input bar stays anchored at the bottom; conversation history scrolls above it
- Clean startup: launches into a fresh screen like a native terminal app
- Clean shutdown: restores the terminal to its original state on Ctrl-C or Ctrl-D

### Streaming Output
- Assistant responses stream token-by-token with a live status line
- Buffered delta ordering fix: all response content is guaranteed to appear before the completion marker — no more truncated or missing output on fast machines

### Multi-Platform Release Pipeline
- GitHub Actions builds native packages for macOS, Linux, and Windows on every version tag
- macOS: DMG installer for Apple Silicon
- Windows: NSIS setup.exe with user-level PATH registration
- Linux: .deb (Debian/Ubuntu), .rpm (RHEL/Fedora), and .tar.gz (universal)
- Each artifact has a matching .sha256 checksum file
- Beta releases are published as GitHub pre-releases; stable releases update the Homebrew tap

### Release Notes System
- `.github/release-notes/<tag>.md` — notes for stable releases
- `.github/beta-notes/<tag>.md` — notes for beta/rc releases (this file)

---

## Download

Go to the **[Releases page](https://github.com/DevEloLin/evoclaw/releases/tag/v1.0.0-beta.1)** and download the artifact for your platform.

### macOS — Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3/M4)

```bash
curl -LO https://github.com/DevEloLin/evoclaw/releases/download/v1.0.0-beta.1/evoclaw-macos-arm64.dmg
shasum -a 256 -c evoclaw-macos-arm64.dmg.sha256
open evoclaw-macos-arm64.dmg
# Drag evoclaw to /usr/local/bin or any directory in your PATH
evoclaw
```

### Linux — Debian / Ubuntu

```bash
curl -LO https://github.com/DevEloLin/evoclaw/releases/download/v1.0.0-beta.1/evoclaw-linux-amd64.deb
sha256sum -c evoclaw-linux-amd64.deb.sha256
sudo dpkg -i evoclaw-linux-amd64.deb
evoclaw
```

### Linux — RHEL / Fedora / CentOS

```bash
curl -LO https://github.com/DevEloLin/evoclaw/releases/download/v1.0.0-beta.1/evoclaw-linux-x86_64.rpm
sha256sum -c evoclaw-linux-x86_64.rpm.sha256
sudo rpm -i evoclaw-linux-x86_64.rpm
evoclaw
```

### Linux — Universal tarball

```bash
curl -LO https://github.com/DevEloLin/evoclaw/releases/download/v1.0.0-beta.1/evoclaw-linux-x86_64.tar.gz
sha256sum -c evoclaw-linux-x86_64.tar.gz.sha256
tar -xzf evoclaw-linux-x86_64.tar.gz
chmod +x evoclaw && sudo mv evoclaw /usr/local/bin/
evoclaw
```

### Windows — x64

```powershell
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "https://github.com/DevEloLin/evoclaw/releases/download/v1.0.0-beta.1/evoclaw-windows-x64-setup.exe" -OutFile evoclaw-setup.exe
Start-Process evoclaw-setup.exe
```

The installer places `evoclaw.exe` in `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\EvoClaw` and adds it to your user PATH. Open a new terminal and run `evoclaw`.

> Homebrew tap tracks stable releases only. For this beta, use the manual install above.

---

## Known Issues

- TUI startup briefly clears the terminal; scrollback from previous sessions is not preserved on launch
- Windows binary is untested on non-English system locales

## Testing Focus

- Interactive REPL: input editing (Ctrl+A/E/K/U/W), history (up/down, Ctrl-P/N), reverse search (Ctrl-R), Tab completion
- Streaming output: long responses, code blocks, fast providers
- macOS DMG mount and binary extraction
- Windows NSIS installer: PATH registration, clean uninstall
- Linux package managers: dpkg, rpm, tar install flows
- Multi-channel adapters: Slack Socket Mode, Telegram long-poll, Discord Gateway WS

---

## Checksums

SHA-256 checksums are provided as `.sha256` files alongside each artifact on the Releases page.
